content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Living Document
Purpose: Routing and maintenance entrypoint for App Behavior Roadmap Templates roadmap templates.
Audience: Agents and engineers selecting, maintaining, or auditing roadmap templates.
Keep In Sync With: agent/ux-system/README.doc.json, local sibling docs, child README files, and changes to this folder's routing role.
Update When: README routing, local files, child folders, template/example status, tool behavior, authority boundaries, or corpus purpose changes.
Does Not Cover: Completed roadmaps, product decisions, App Behavior truth, architecture authority, implementation plans, proof results, or source facts.
Purpose: provide derived roadmap templates for planning App Behavior authority without replacing the governing App Behavior specs, schemas, model docs, or user-confirmed product decisions.
Use these templates when a human and agent need to see whether App Behavior planning is ordered, confirmed, reopened, blocked, or ready to hand off to architecture, implementation planning, or proof.

content.title: Routing
body: Read `01-app-behavior-roadmap-template.doc.json` for the top-level App Behavior flow.
Use `02` through `05` for behavior package, current/desired/governing, runtime drift, and pending product-decision sub-roadmaps.
Files with `.placeholder.doc.json` are intentional placeholders and are not complete roadmap templates.

content.title: Authority Boundary And Stop Condition
body: App Behavior roadmap templates are starting shapes for later App Behavior corpus work. They do not create behavior truth, replace source tickets/runtime evidence, or substitute for final App Behavior authority.
Stop when the agent can choose the App Behavior roadmap template, name the future behavior artifact it will seed, and state which source authority or evidence must still be used.

content.title: Template Rules
body: Every full roadmap template in this folder uses or inherits these fields: identity, status, parent roadmap, child roadmaps, mission, pillars/core concepts, authority inputs, derived summary, ordered steps, confirmation gates, reopened items, open decisions, next recommended step, stop condition, and non-authority warning.

Every placeholder roadmap template should include status `placeholder`, purpose, why the template likely belongs, what is already decision-complete, what needs clarity, authority to review before completion, promotion criteria, possible outcomes, and non-authority warning.
Roadmap phases and meaningful subphases should include `review_before_moving_forward`.
Use `agent/core-workflows.doc.json` for standard `.core.*` review ladders, and use `specific_review` for active local docs, artifacts, specs, source evidence, or workstream files.
When a roadmap phase or meaningful subphase appears complete, run `.core.audit` before moving forward.
After `.core.audit`, offer another `.core.audit` pass before advancing.
The audit gate confirms phase readiness; it does not replace the phase’s own `review_before_moving_forward`.
